title: ASTRO 1110 Program Review Fall 2018 (Part 1)
text:
College of the Siskiyous Annual Program Update
Program: Astronomy 1110 (Fall 2018) Date: 13-Dec-2018
Faculty Completing Form: Patrick McDougall
1. Describe annual progress / changes / events in the program.
a. The last time this course was offered was during the 2016 calendar year. Due to the delay in
time the course has been offered, the instructor will be loosely using the data from 2016 as a
comparison for program evaluation.
b. Enrollment comparison: The spring semester had an enrollment of 10 students with 9 finishing
the term (90 % retention), the fall semester had an enrollment of 11 students with 10 finishing
the term (90.91% retention). During the fall of 2018, the initial enrollment was 25 students with
23 finishing the term (92% retention). As of this date (12/13/18) the enrollment spring 2019 is
27 students. This shows a continuation of students being interested in the topic.
c. At this point the instructor has not entered final grades for the fall of 2018. Due to this, a
complete and honest comparison of student success cannot be made.
d. This class was run as a three-hour lecture once a week. The lecture time was broken into the
following pattern: One-hour lecture on first topic, ten-minute break, one-hour lecture second
topic, ten-minute break, one-hour activity and discussion about material. The last hour is setup
as more of a “show don’t tell” form of teaching. The activities and discussions allow students to
dive further into the material and clarify any questions before they leave to do homework for
the week. This schedule will be changed starting in the spring of 2019 due to the change in times
met for the class. The essence of this structure will remain even with the changes in schedule.
2. Describe progress on assessing SLOs and PLOs for the program.
a. Next term ASTRO 1110 will focus on “Analyze whether an observation, experimental result or
proposed explanation is consistent with a scientific hypothesis for a natural phenomenon and
effectively communicate this analysis to others.” This will be done by examining different
explanations of observed phenomenon with different explanations. Based on data collected
over history, students will be able to come to their own conclusion about what the explanation
is. This evaluation will not depend on the students coming to the correct conclusion, it will only
depend of how they can inform the reader about their results. After each assignment is due, the
instructor will review the data and show how the scientific community has come to current
conclusion.
3. Discuss any support or obstacles encountered by the program.
a. During the fall of 2018, there were a few requests for materials summing under $100. These
materials were supplied and ready for class.
4. Describe program budgetary needs or implications. (Attach completed CQIP form.)
a. ASTRO 1110 currently has one telescope. This telescope is great for viewing the night sky, the
moon, and the Sun (using a solar filter). However, this telescope does not meet the
requirements for viewing planets. The instructor is currently in talks with members of the
community about telescope donations. If those do not come through, the instructor will reach
out about getting a new telescope.
